title: Define Zephyr HAL contract and ownership boundaries
description: Why this exists: LICHEN needs a clear HAL boundary that application/protocol code can rely on across LilyGO, Heltec, RAK, STM32WL, RP2040, nRF52840, ESP32-class, gateway, and headless targets. What needs to be done: define the C API, subsystem ownership, initialization ordering, thread-safety expectations, error model, and capability discovery surface for board services including radio, display, input buttons, LEDs, GNSS/location, date/time, battery/PMIC, storage, BLE/serial/local-client transport, board identity, and reset/diagnostic controls. The contract must state which APIs are mandatory, optional, stubbed, or compile-time-disabled. Acceptance criteria: public headers and design notes describe each HAL service; no application code needs board-specific #ifdefs for the covered services; missing capability behavior is explicit and testable; initialization dependencies align with AGENTS.md subsystem order; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Progress from project-LICHEN-ev5b.3: added initial minimal lichen_hal subsystem and public API for board capabilities, identity, deterministic unsupported/unavailable behavior, radio/GNSS/display/serial/battery/PMIC/external flash device lookup, LED/button GPIO specs, and BLE local status. This is enough for R1/R1 Neo MVP board target work but does not close h7t5.1 because broader design notes, initialization/thread-safety documentation, reset/diagnostic controls, and app code de-ifdefing remain.

Input from project-LICHEN-h7t5.5 HAL-pattern evaluation: keep Meshtastic/MeshCore as board-knowledge and smoke-matrix references, but keep LICHEN Zephyr-native. The HAL contract should encode board capabilities via devicetree chosen/alias nodes, Kconfig capability symbols, deterministic status/error APIs, and board-local quirks near board definitions. Avoid importing PlatformIO env inheritance, Arduino setup/loop globals, variant header macro sprawl, or RadioLib-specific types into application/protocol boundaries. This recommendation has already shaped the HAL capability API, board capability matrix, and app migration away from board ifdefs.
